raw: {"confidence":0.76,"critical_failures":{"A":[],"B":[]},"generic_mechanism":"Belief changes more when each chapter causally reinterprets familiar experience; clarifying scope and inviting doubt reduce resistance, but cannot replace testing the behavior’s supposed payoff.","paraphrased_evidence":{"A":"Withdrawal relief, rebound hunger, conditioned taste and reward, and restriction’s backlash recast consumption as maintaining its own cycle; repeated promises and sweeping analogies weaken the case.","B":"The chapters distinguish effortless release from managed abstinence, narrow the target, and expose inherited expectations, but repeatedly defer testing whether pleasure, comfort, and reward are actually delivered, leaving the central benefits substantially intact."},"product_parity_verdict":"A","scores":{"belief_movement":{"A":6,"B":5},"benefit_dismantling":{"A":7,"B":4},"cumulative_progression":{"A":6,"B":7},"escape_conviction":{"A":6,"B":7}}}

parsed.generic_mechanism: Belief changes more when each chapter causally reinterprets familiar experience; clarifying scope and inviting doubt reduce resistance, but cannot replace testing the behavior’s supposed payoff.

paraphrased_evidence.A: Withdrawal relief, rebound hunger, conditioned taste and reward, and restriction’s backlash recast consumption as maintaining its own cycle; repeated promises and sweeping analogies weaken the case.
paraphrased_evidence.B: The chapters distinguish effortless release from managed abstinence, narrow the target, and expose inherited expectations, but repeatedly defer testing whether pleasure, comfort, and reward are actually delivered, leaving the central benefits substantially intact.

paraphrased_evidence.ours: The chapters distinguish effortless release from managed abstinence, narrow the target, and expose inherited expectations, but repeatedly defer testing whether pleasure, comfort, and reward are actually delivered, leaving the central benefits substantially intact.
paraphrased_evidence.ref: Withdrawal relief, rebound hunger, conditioned taste and reward, and restriction’s backlash recast consumption as maintaining its own cycle; repeated promises and sweeping analogies weaken the case.
